data.manual_ignore_intermediate: Ignore intermediate positions during manual override (opening and closing)
data.return_sunset: Always adjust position to sunset default at end time; Useful if end time is before actual sunset

data_description.delta_position: Minimum change in position required before adjusting the cover's position
data_description.delta_time: Minimum time interval between position changes; minimum is 2 minutes
data_description.start_time: Starting time for each day; before this time, the cover will remain stationary
data_description.start_entity: Entity representing starting time state, overriding the fixed start time set above. Useful for automating the start time with an entity
data_description.manual_override_duration: The duration of manual control before resetting to automatic control
data_description.manual_override_reset: Option to reset the manual override duration after each manual adjustment; if disabled, the duration only applies to the first manual adjustment
data_description.end_time: Ending time for each day; after this time, the cover will remain stationary
data_description.end_entity: Entity representing ending time state, overriding the fixed end time set above. Useful for automating the end time with an entity

data.enable_min_position: Only force the minimum position when the sun is in front of the window
data.enable_max_position: Only force the maximum position when the sun is in front of the window
data.inverse_state: Inverse the state (needed for some covers that don't follow HA guidelines)
data.interp: Set custom open/close positions for My Cover, if it doesn't fully operate at 0-100%

data_description.facade_name: Facade or house preset group for this window.
data_description.floor_name: Select the Home Assistant floor. Used for house-level defaults and later grouped edits.
data_description.room_name: Select the Home Assistant area/room. Used for room-level defaults and later grouped edits.
data_description.distance_shaded_area: Legacy base-geometry parameter from Adaptive Cover. It is less important for the new daylight-first heat-protection policy and will likely be removed later.
data_description.default_percentage: Fallback cover position, where 100% open means fully open and 0% open means closed.
data_description.min_position: Minimum adjustable cover position as a percentage
data_description.max_position: Maximum adjustable cover position as a percentage
data_description.enable_min_position: Only enforce the minimum position while the sun is actually relevant for this window.
data_description.enable_max_position: Only enforce the maximum position while the sun is actually relevant for this window.
data_description.fov_left: Field of view angle to the left of the window center
data_description.fov_right: Field of view angle to the right of the window center
data_description.group: Select entities to control via integration
data_description.sunset_position: Position used outside the active time/sun window. Sunset and sunrise offsets decide when night mode begins and ends; set 100% open to stay open at night.
data_description.sunset_offset: Offset (?) from sunset time in minutes
data_description.sunrise_offset: Offset (?) from sunrise time in minutes
data_description.window_width: Visible glass width in meters. Required when reveal depths are used.
data_description.reveal_left_depth: Left reveal depth in meters. Increases shading when the sun comes from the left.
data_description.reveal_right_depth: Right reveal depth in meters. Increases shading when the sun comes from the right.
data_description.reveal_top_depth: Top reveal depth in meters. Increases shading when the sun is high.
data_description.horizon_profile: Optional JSON list of local sky limits from 0 to 180 degrees. Open the horizon drawer/preview at /local/solar_shading_horizon_preview.html or use the integrated drawer in /local/solar_shading_simulator.html, then paste the JSON here.
data_description.glass_type: Glazing preset used for reflection and transmission.
data_description.weather_entity: Used only for outside temperature and today/tomorrow maximum temperature forecasts.

vertical.description: Configuration variables for the cover. Edit visually in the simulator: /local/solar_shading_simulator.html. Standalone horizon drawer: /local/solar_shading_horizon_preview.html.
vertical.title: Vertical cover

data.outside_threshold: Minimum outside temperature for summer mode

data_description.presence_entity: Entity representing room or home presence status
data_description.weather_entity: Provides outside temperature and today's/tomorrow's maximum temperature forecast only
data_description.outside_temp: Overrides outside temperature from weather entity if both are set
data_description.transparent_blind: The blind is transparent and should always close fully when summer mode is active
data_description.irradiance_entity: Use irradiance sensor to determine if cover should reduce glare in presence mode
data_description.irradiance_threshold: Threshold for irradiance sensor, above this value glare should be reduced
data_description.outside_threshold: Outside temperature above which summer-mode behavior may activate

data_description.use_forecast_max_temp_today: Enable today's forecast maximum temperature as a hot-day trigger
data_description.use_forecast_max_temp_tomorrow: Enable tomorrow's forecast maximum temperature as an early hot-day signal
data_description.forecast_hot_day_threshold: Forecast maximum temperature where preemptive hot-day handling begins
data_description.forecast_very_hot_day_threshold: Forecast maximum temperature where hot-day risk reaches its maximum
data_description.forecast_preemptive_start_time: Earliest local time when forecast-based preemptive shading may activate
data_description.forecast_influence_strength: How strongly the forecast risk can amplify the effective solar gain factor
data_description.use_open_data_solar_radiation: Fetch direct solar irradiance in W/m2 from Open-Meteo for the Home Assistant location. Clouds and precipitation are already reflected in the irradiance and are not scored separately.
data_description.solar_radiation_entity: Optional local irradiance sensor in W/m2. If set, it overrides Open-Meteo for the current irradiance factor; Open-Meteo can still provide forecast radiation.
data_description.solar_radiation_reference_w_m2: Incoming direct irradiance before window geometry and glazing that counts as strong sun. Current W/m2 divided by this threshold becomes the normalized irradiance factor; lower values react more aggressively.

weather.title: Solar irradiance and temperature forecast
weather.description: Solar irradiance drives the current heat input. Only today's and tomorrow's maximum temperatures are retained from the weather forecast.

data.has_additional_daylight_windows: Additional daylight windows on other orientations
data.heat_protection_min_outside_temp: Disable heat protection below outside temperature

data_description.enable_heat_gain_policy: Turns the daylight-aware heat-gain overlay on top of the base shading logic on or off.
data_description.policy_preset: Preset daylight/cooling bias. In normal mode it also provides the expert weights; enable expert weights to override them manually.
data_description.has_additional_daylight_windows: If the room has relevant windows on other orientations, Solar Shading may close this window a bit more aggressively.
data_description.weight_direct_exposure: Weight of the geometric exposure factor from horizon and reveals. Set to 0 to disable this factor.
data_description.weight_incidence: Weight of the angle between sun and window normal. Set to 0 to ignore shallow-vs-direct incidence.
data_description.weight_glazing: Weight of the glazing transmission model. Set to 0 to ignore glass-related differences.
data_description.weight_forecast_temperature: Expert weight for hot-day forecast as a boost. Below the hot-day threshold it is neutral and no longer disables heat protection by itself.
data_description.partial_close_threshold: Weighted heat-gain score above which the cover may become less open than the base calculation.
data_description.full_close_threshold: Weighted heat-gain score above which the cover should move to the stricter open target.
data_description.partial_close_position: Open-position target at the partial threshold. 100% open is fully open, 0% open is closed.
data_description.full_close_position: Minimum open position for normal policy control. Presets, away mode, and very-hot pressure will not close below this value; only explicit overrides can.
data_description.enable_away_mode: Enables stricter heat protection whenever the selected entity indicates nobody is home.
data_description.away_entity: Entity used to detect away status. For person/device_tracker every state except home counts as away. For binary_sensor/input_boolean, off counts as away.
data_description.away_score_multiplier: Raises the final heat-gain score while away and therefore makes the reaction stricter.
data_description.away_threshold_reduction: Lowers the partial/full close thresholds while away.
data_description.away_position_offset: Reduces the allowed open position while away, making shading stricter.
data_description.hot_day_close_enabled: If enabled, a forecast above the threshold can cap the open position even outside the finer weighted policy.
data_description.hot_day_close_threshold: Forecast temperature above which the day is treated as generally hot.
data_description.hot_day_close_position: Maximum open position allowed on a generally hot day. Lower values mean more shading.
data_description.very_hot_day_close_position: Maximum open position allowed once the forecast reaches the very-hot threshold. Use a lower value than the hot-day maximum for visibly stricter behavior.
data_description.show_expert_weights: Show and use individual policy weights. When disabled, the selected preset provides the weights.
data_description.weight_solar_radiation: Expert weight for measured or open-data solar radiation in W/m2. Set to 0 to ignore this factor in the policy score.
data_description.heat_power_limit_enabled: When enabled, Solar Shading calculates transmitted solar power from incoming irradiance, geometry, incidence angle and glazing. It caps the open position when the W/m2 limit is exceeded on a hot forecast day or above the outside-temperature threshold.
data_description.heat_power_outside_temp_threshold: Outside temperature from which the heat-power cap may intervene. On forecast hot or very-hot days, the hard watt cap may intervene before this temperature is reached.
data_description.heat_protection_min_outside_temp: Below this measured outside temperature, heat protection is disabled even when the forecast is hot or very hot.
data_description.max_transmitted_solar_power_w_m2: Maximum allowed solar power transmitted through one square meter of window glass. The open position is reduced approximately linearly to stay below this value.

policy.title: Heat protection presets and fine tuning
policy.description: All weights, heat thresholds, % open targets, and stricter away-from-home behavior for the daylight-aware heat-gain overlay live here. Weight 0 disables an individual factor. Simulator for fine-tuning: /local/solar_shading_simulator.html

data.enable_max_position: Only force the maximum position when the sun is in front of the window
data.inverse_state: Inverse the state (needed for some covers that don't follow HA guidelines)

data_description.weather_entity: Used only for outside temperature and today/tomorrow maximum temperature forecasts.
data_description.distance_shaded_area: Legacy base-geometry parameter from Adaptive Cover. It is less important for the new daylight-first heat-protection policy and will likely be removed later.

tilt.description: Configuration variables for the cover. Edit visually in the simulator: /local/solar_shading_simulator.html. Standalone horizon drawer: /local/solar_shading_horizon_preview.html.
tilt.title: Tilted cover

data_description.blind_spot_elevation: Any value equal or less is considered within the blindspot. Usefull if the sun can shine over the object in the blindspot

blind_spot.title: Blindspot
blind_spot.description: Setup blindspot for the cover. These values are relative degrees to the width of the field of view. Where 0 is the leftmost position taken from the field of view left parameter.

data_description.interp_list: List of interpolation values, where 0 is fully closed and 100 is fully open
data_description.interp_list_new: Custom range of interpolation values, where the beginning should represent closed and the end open

interp.title: Range Adjustment
interp.description:
Adjust the values at which your Cover effectively opens or closes. Ideal for Covers that don't fully open at 0% or close at 100%. 
 The first two sliders are for a 2 point adjustment, the list options are for a multiple point adjustment

error.window_width_required_for_reveals: Window width is required when reveal depths are set.
error.full_threshold_must_exceed_partial: The strict heat-protection threshold must be greater than or equal to the partial threshold.
error.full_position_must_exceed_partial: The strict open target must be lower than or equal to the partial open target.
error.full_open_position_must_be_lower: The strict open target must be lower than or equal to the partial open target.
error.away_entity_required: An away entity must be selected when away-from-home mode is enabled.

data.manual_ignore_intermediate: Ignore intermediate positions during manual override (opening and closing)
data.return_sunset: Always adjust position to sunset default at end time; Useful if end time is before actual sunset

data_description.end_time: Ending time for each day; after this time, the cover will remain stationary
data_description.end_entity: Ensure that the position is consistently adjusted to the default sunset setting by the end time. This is particularly beneficial when the end time precedes the actual sunset.

data_description.weather_entity: Used only for outside temperature and today/tomorrow maximum temperature forecasts.

horizontal.description: Configuration variables for the cover. Edit visually in the simulator: /local/solar_shading_simulator.html. Standalone horizon drawer: /local/solar_shading_horizon_preview.html.
horizontal.title: Horizontal cover

data.enable_min_position: Only force the minimum position when the sun is in front of the window
data.enable_max_position: Only force the maximum position when the sun is in front of the window
data.inverse_state: Inverse the state (needed for some covers that don't follow HA guidelines)
data.interp: Set custom open/close positions for My Cover, if it doesn't fully operate at 0-100%

data_description.facade_name: Facade or house preset group for this window.
data_description.floor_name: Select the Home Assistant floor. Used for house-level defaults and later grouped edits.
data_description.room_name: Select the Home Assistant area/room. Used for room-level defaults and later grouped edits.
data_description.slat_depth: Depth of each individual slat in centimeters
data_description.slat_distance: Vertical distance between two slats in centimeters
data_description.default_percentage: Fallback cover position, where 100% open means fully open and 0% open means closed.
data_description.min_position: Minimum adjustable cover position as a percentage
data_description.max_position: Maximum adjustable cover position as a percentage
data_description.enable_min_position: Only enforce the minimum position while the sun is actually relevant for this window.
data_description.enable_max_position: Only enforce the maximum position while the sun is actually relevant for this window.
data_description.fov_left: Field of view angle to the left of the window center
data_description.fov_right: Field of view angle to the right of the window center
data_description.group: Select entities to control via integration
data_description.sunset_position: Position used outside the active time/sun window. Sunset and sunrise offsets decide when night mode begins and ends; set 100% open to stay open at night.
data_description.sunset_offset: Offset (?) from sunset time in minutes
data_description.sunrise_offset: Offset (?) from sunrise time in minutes
data_description.window_width: Visible glass width in meters. Required when reveal depths are used.
data_description.reveal_left_depth: Left reveal depth in meters. Increases shading when the sun comes from the left.
data_description.reveal_right_depth: Right reveal depth in meters. Increases shading when the sun comes from the right.
data_description.reveal_top_depth: Top reveal depth in meters. Increases shading when the sun is high.
data_description.horizon_profile: Optional JSON list of local sky limits from 0 to 180 degrees. Open the horizon drawer/preview at /local/solar_shading_horizon_preview.html or use the integrated drawer in /local/solar_shading_simulator.html, then paste the JSON here.
data_description.glass_type: Glazing preset used for reflection and transmission.
data_description.weather_entity: Used only for outside temperature and today/tomorrow maximum temperature forecasts.
data_description.distance_shaded_area: Legacy base-geometry parameter from Adaptive Cover. It is less important for the new daylight-first heat-protection policy and will likely be removed later.

tilt.description: Configuration variables for the cover. Edit visually in the simulator: /local/solar_shading_simulator.html. Standalone horizon drawer: /local/solar_shading_horizon_preview.html.
tilt.title: Tilted cover

data_description.blind_spot_elevation: Any value equal or less is considered within the blindspot. Usefull if the sun can shine over the object in the blindspot

blind_spot.title: Blindspot
blind_spot.description: Setup blindspot for the cover. These values are relative degrees to the width of the field of view. Where 0 is the leftmost position taken from the field of view left parameter.

data_description.interp_list: List of interpolation values, where 0 is fully closed and 100 is fully open
data_description.interp_list_new: Custom range of interpolation values, where the beginning should represent closed and the end open

interp.title: Range Adjustment
interp.description:
Adjust the values at which your Cover effectively opens or closes. Ideal for Covers that don't fully open at 0% or close at 100%. 
 The first two sliders are for a 2 point adjustment, the list options are for a multiple point adjustment
